x-enumeration-literals.name: Regulatory Adherence Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that defines how a product or service complies with applicable laws, regulations, and regulatory frameworks.

Comment:
Focus: External obligations

Includes:
AML / CTF requirements
Sanctions compliance
Tax regulations (e.g., CRS, FATCA)
Data protection laws

x-enumeration-literals.name: Customer Compliance Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that defines the obligations and responsibilities of the customer to enable regulatory compliance.

Comment:
Focus: Customer duties

Includes:
KYC / KYB information provision
Representations and warranties (e.g., not sanctioned)
Ongoing disclosure obligations
Notification of changes (ownership, control, status)

x-enumeration-literals.name: Monitoring & Control Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that establishes the mechanisms and rules for monitoring activities and detecting non-compliance or risk.

Comment:
Focus: Detection and prevention

Includes
Transaction monitoring rules
Risk scoring models
Alert generation and investigation triggers
Fraud and AML detection controls

x-enumeration-literals.name: Reporting & Regulatory Interaction Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that governs how and when information is reported to regulators and how interactions with authorities are handled.

Comment:
Focus: Regulatory communication

Includes:
Suspicious Activity Reports (SAR/STR)
Regulatory disclosures and filings
Cooperation with authorities
Information-sharing obligations

x-enumeration-literals.name: Third-Party Compliance Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that defines how third-party providers are used to support compliance processes and how data is shared with them.

Comment:
Focus: Outsourcing and external support

Includes:
Identity verification services
Sanctions screening providers
Fraud/AML platforms
Outsourcing terms and responsibilities

x-enumeration-literals.name: Audit & Assurance Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that ensures compliance activities are documented, traceable, and verifiable through audits and reviews.

Comment:
Focus: Evidence and verification

Includes
Record-keeping requirements
Audit rights and obligations
Compliance reviews
Documentation and traceability

x-enumeration-literals.name: Enforcement & Remediation Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that defines the actions, rights, and processes applied when compliance risks or breaches occur.

Comment:
Focus: Response to non-compliance

Includes
Transaction refusal or blocking
Account suspension or termination
Remediation actions
Liability and risk mitigation clauses

x-enumeration-literals.name: Data Governance & Privacy Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
An arrangement that governs how data is collected, processed, shared, and retained in compliance with data protection laws.

Comment:
Focus: Data compliance

Includes:
Data usage for compliance purposes
Data sharing with regulators and third parties
Retention and deletion policies
Cross-border data transfers
